title: Meta to put AI chip into production in September as it looks to double computing capacity, memo shows
text:
Daniel Nenni
Founder
Staff member

Meta Platforms plans to begin producing a new in-house artificial intelligence chip in September, a move that underscores how aggressively the company is racing to expand its AI infrastructure. According to a Reuters report citing an internal memo, the chip is code-named Iris and is part of Meta’s broader MTIA program, which aims to reduce dependence on outside suppliers while improving the efficiency of AI workloads across Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp and future products.

The chip is expected to support Meta’s goal of doubling its computing capacity to 14 gigawatts next year. That target reflects the enormous power and hardware demands behind generative AI, recommendation systems, advertising tools and large-scale model training. Meta has spent heavily on data centers, GPUs and custom silicon as it competes with OpenAI, Google, Microsoft and Amazon.

If successful, Iris could help Meta control costs and secure capacity in a market where advanced AI chips remain expensive and supply-constrained. Still, custom chip programs are difficult, and Meta will likely continue relying on Nvidia and other suppliers for years to meet the biggest training needs. The September production plan signals that Meta sees chip independence as central to its long-term AI strategy, not just a cost-saving experiment.
